sections.heading: Payment of Minimum Wages
sections.body_text: The appropriate government shall fix the minimum rates of wages payable to employees in scheduled employments, ensuring that no worker is paid less than the minimum wage. The rates may be fixed for time work, piece work, or overtime.

sections.heading: Payment of Wages
sections.body_text: Every employer shall be responsible for the payment of wages to employees employed by them. Wages must be paid without unauthorized deductions and within the prescribed time limits.

sections.heading: Payment of Bonus
sections.body_text: Every employer shall pay an annual bonus to employees who have worked for at least 30 days in an accounting year, provided the establishment meets the eligibility criteria for bonus payment.

sections.heading: Retrenchment Compensation
sections.body_text: An employer shall provide compensation to a workman who has been in continuous service for not less than one year before retrenchment. The compensation shall be equivalent to 15 days' average pay for every completed year of service.
sections.chapter: Chapter X - Lay-Off, Retrenchment, and Closure

sections.heading: Resolution of Industrial Disputes
sections.body_text: Industrial disputes shall be resolved through conciliation, arbitration, or adjudication by the appropriate authorities, including conciliation officers, tribunals, and national tribunals.

sections.heading: Prohibition of Strikes and Lockouts
sections.body_text: No person employed in an industrial establishment shall go on strike, and no employer shall declare a lockout without giving prior notice as prescribed under the Code.

sections.heading: Social Security Benefits - Provident Fund
sections.body_text: The Code mandates the establishment of a provident fund for employees, requiring contributions from both employers and employees, to provide financial security post-retirement.
sections.chapter: Chapter IV - Social Security and Provident Fund

sections.heading: Social Security Benefits - Employees' State Insurance
sections.body_text: The Code provides for the establishment of the Employees' State Insurance Corporation to administer benefits such as medical care, sickness benefits, and maternity benefits to insured employees.

sections.heading: Gratuity
sections.body_text: Gratuity shall be payable to an employee who has rendered continuous service for not less than five years, upon termination of employment due to superannuation, retirement, resignation, or death.

sections.heading: Duties of Employers
sections.body_text: Employers are required to ensure a safe working environment, provide necessary safety equipment, and comply with prescribed safety standards to protect workers from occupational hazards.
sections.chapter: Chapter II - Duties of Employers and Employees

sections.heading: Occupational Safety and Health Standards
sections.body_text: The Code mandates the formulation and implementation of occupational safety and health standards to ensure the safety, health, and welfare of workers in various industries.
sections.chapter: Chapter IV - Safety and Working Conditions

sections.heading: Health and Working Conditions
sections.body_text: Employers must ensure adequate ventilation, lighting, cleanliness, and other health and safety measures in the workplace to protect workers' well-being.
sections.chapter: Chapter IV - Safety and Working Conditions

sections.heading: Fixed Term Employment
sections.body_text: The Code allows for fixed-term employment contracts, ensuring that fixed-term employees receive the same benefits as permanent employees, including gratuity, if they complete one year of service.
sections.chapter: Chapter V - Terms and Conditions of Employment

sections.heading: Timely Payment of Wages
sections.body_text: Wages must be paid in a timely manner, as prescribed by the appropriate government, and no deductions shall be made except as authorized by the Code.

sections.heading: Notice of Change in Employment Conditions
sections.body_text: Employers must provide notice to workers before making any changes to employment conditions, such as wages, hours of work, or other terms of service.

sections.heading: Maternity Benefits
sections.body_text: The Code provides for maternity benefits, including paid leave, medical bonus, and nursing breaks, to female employees who meet the eligibility criteria.

sections.heading: Unorganized Workers' Social Security
sections.body_text: The Code mandates the formulation of schemes to provide social security benefits to unorganized workers, including life and disability cover, health benefits, and old-age protection.
sections.chapter: Chapter VIII - Social Security for Unorganized Workers

sections.heading: Welfare Provisions
sections.body_text: Employers are required to provide welfare facilities such as canteens, restrooms, and first-aid facilities to ensure the well-being of workers.
sections.chapter: Chapter IV - Safety and Working Conditions

sections.heading: Prohibition of Sexual Harassment
sections.body_text: Employers must ensure that the workplace is free from sexual harassment and must implement measures to prevent and address complaints of harassment.
sections.chapter: Chapter V - Terms and Conditions of Employment

sections.heading: Penalties for Non-Compliance
sections.body_text: The Code prescribes penalties for employers who fail to comply with its provisions, including fines and imprisonment, depending on the nature and severity of the violation.
